description_long: This is a vintage mechanical cash register, predominantly off-white with a slightly weathered appearance. The front features a vertical array of black and white keys, each labeled with numbers and letters, as well as a few red and white function buttons. A prominent metal lever is attached to the right side, used for operating the register, and a cash drawer with multiple compartments is open at the base, revealing currency slots. The top section includes a rectangular display window, likely for showing transaction totals, and several brand labels reading 'TITCH BANKS' and 'TITCH CO.' are visible. The overall construction appears robust, with a metal body and plastic or bakelite keys, and the design is utilitarian, typical of mid-20th-century registers. The back is plain, with only the brand label and a few screws visible.
description: A vintage off-white mechanical cash register with a vertical keypad, side lever, and open cash drawer, featuring 'TITCH BANKS' branding and a utilitarian metal body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keypad, display window, open cash drawer with compartments, and visible brand labels.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the large operating lever, the angled keypad, and the open cash drawer extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain surface with the 'TITCH BANKS' label and no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the side profile of the keypad, the open cash drawer, and the absence of a lever on this side.
attribute_keypad_layout: Vertical grid of approximately 20 rectangular keys, labeled with numbers, letters, and function symbols.
